content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

February 8, 2005

Stephen P. Pfanler, City Attorney
Bannan, Green, Frank & Terzian, LLP
555 South Flower Street, Suite 2700
Los Angeles, CA 90071

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-04-250

Dear Mr. Pfanler:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding South Pasadena City Councilmember David Margrave on the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to any member or employee whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized representative. (Section 83114(b); regulation 18329(a) - copy enclosed.) However, the Commission may also decline to provide written advice if it would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to do so. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(F).)

Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by the requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(b)(7).) After a careful review of the facts you submitted, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division. Therefore, it would be inappropriate for the Commission to provide advice on this matter while the enforcement investigation is still pending.

Our decision is not a determination of the questions posed in your request. In addition, nothing in the Act prevents an official from seeking legal advice from you or private counsel, during our investigation. If the Enforcement Division resolves the matter with a finding that no violation has occurred, you may resubmit your request for advice at that time.

___________
1. Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 – 18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

Advice File No. W-04-250
Page No. 2

If you have any questions, please contact the Commission at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

William J. Lankelt
Commission Counsel
Legal Division

Enclosure
WJL:jg

The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division. Therefore, it would be inappropriate for the Commission to provide advice on this matter while the enforcement investigation is still pending.

sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide written advice because the same or similar issues are the subject of a pending Enforcement Division investigation, making it inappropriate to advise at this time. The requestor may resubmit the request after the investigation is resolved.

No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with explicit headers are present, but the substance of the request and the declination reasoning are clearly stated in the body text. File number W-04-250 indicates this is a written advice request file.

QUESTION: Whether South Pasadena City Councilmember David Margrave complies with the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: After a careful review of the facts you submitted, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division. Therefore, it would be inappropriate for the Commission to provide advice on this matter while the enforcement investigation is still pending.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide written advice regarding South Pasadena City Councilmember David Margrave's compliance with conflict-of-interest provisions because the same issues are under active investigation by the Enforcement Division.
